proposed_html: The contradiction stated up front. κ is the dome's structural coupling constant — computed as 1.67 nT/µGal from −10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, October 2003 G5 Halloween storm, Dst = −383 nT) divided by −6.5 µGal (Mohe spring gravimeter, March 1997 eclipse). The dome's claim, "Magnetic-gravity coupling constant is 1.67 × 10−10," is this same ratio in SI units. κ also drives the dome's forward gravity prediction (PRED-TIER-3), so abandoning κ destroys the prediction the dome wants to make. Three-step refutation. (1) The Mohe −6.5 µGal anomaly has not been independently replicated — the dome's own WIN-011 now labels the Mohe mechanism "CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)" and concedes Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) ruled out the original gravitational-shielding interpretation, leaving thermal-tilt artifact as a live alternative. (2) Subsequent eclipse gravity measurements with superior superconducting gravimeters returned null: 0.0 µGal at Membach during the August 1999 eclipse (Van Camp et al., 2001) and across a five-station Chinese SG network during the 2009 eclipse (Sun et al., 2010, Phys. Earth Planet. Int.). (3) These nulls are the dome's own WIN-013 and WIN-014. If they're correct, the denominator of κ is zero and κ is undefined. The numerator is a single storm event, not a quiet-day baseline. −10.9 nT is the BOU disturbed-day excursion during one G5 event — fitted to one extreme datum with no demonstrated broad applicability. Even if WIN-013/014 didn't already void the ratio, the storm-event numerator would. The derivation is also circular. The numerator (BOU 2017 storm) and denominator (Mohe 1997 eclipse) come from different eclipses, different locations, different instrument classes — no single event measured both quantities co-located. A genuine coupling constant requires simultaneous measurement of both coupled quantities at the same location during the same event. WIN-011's mechanism concession destroys κ even granting the number. If, per the dome's current WIN-011, the Mohe −6.5 µGal signal is plausibly a thermal-tilt artifact of the LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter responding to ambient temperature transients (rather than a gravitational response to lunar mass), then the κ denominator is not a gravity measurement at all — it is instrument noise. A coupling between a magnetic field and an instrument artifact is not a physical coupling. The dome cannot simultaneously concede in WIN-011 that the Mohe mechanism is thermal-tilt-plausible and continue to use the same −6.5 µGal number in WIN-012 as the gravity-side denominator of a "magnetic-gravity" coupling. The defender-rescues all destroy PRED-TIER-3. Every retreat the dome has constructed — that κ doesn't apply to 1999/2009, that the Chebychev pipeline filters contact-phase transients before Level-3 release, that the mechanism switched from gravitational shielding to dielectric boundary transition between 1999 and 2026, that the SG campaigns "weren't optimized for C1/C4 timing" — carries the same cost. Either κ applies generally (in which case 1999/2009 refute it) or κ has unspecified scope restrictions (in which case PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation). The structural vise is documented at Part 6.14, The κ Coupling Cluster, which also covers the storm-corollary version (WIN-058b) and the cluster-wide cross-references. Cross-references. For Mohe-anomaly quality and the thermal-tilt mechanism debate, see WIN-011. For the structural vise (κ applies vs PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation), the storm corollary, and the dome's published-mechanism options going forward, see Part 6.14 κ Cluster. For the empirical refutations themselves, see WIN-013 (Membach 1999) and WIN-014 (Chinese SG network 2009).
rationale: Per HNOTE compression experiment to probe whether real content compression (not just sentence-reformatting like RW-001) is viable for a 1888-word over-target WIN. Strategy: lead with the structural framing (kappa is load-bearing for PRED-TIER-3), then deliver the 3-step refutation chain in one paragraph, then keep the WIN-011 mechanism-concession arm (it's the strongest single argument), then summarize the defender-rescue branch as one paragraph pointing to Part 6.14 for the full structural vise. Cut: the GRACE L1A/Exhibit-A graph forensics (entire argument, ~270 words), the monitor.py tautology paragraph (~80 words), the detailed Chebychev filter spec discussion (~200 words), the detailed dielectric-boundary-transition magnitude-mismatch / self-filtering-detector / parameter-adoption walkthrough (~400 words), the detailed Yang & Wang reanalysis citation (~80 words), the Self-Contradiction-with-WIN-013/014 retcon walkthrough (~180 words). The retcon walkthrough's punchline (incompatibility between PRED-TIER-2's 10-13 uGal vs Mohe's -6.5 uGal) moves to Part 6.14 where it lives alongside the storm-corollary version. The Exhibit-A graph forensics is structurally a *separate* argument (about the dome's home-page chart being fabricated rather than about kappa being undefined), which is worth keeping as its own argument but doesn't belong inside WIN-012's detail_evidence — propose a follow-up issue to give it its own WIN or section block.

Full argument lives in Part 6.14 vise subsection.

The numerical incompatibility (-10.2 to -12.6 uGal from PRED-TIER-2 / kappa, incompatible with the -6.5 uGal Mohe anchor) belongs in Part 6.14 vise subsection where the forward-prediction-derivation lives.

follow_up_issue_proposed.proposal: The GRACE L1A / Exhibit-A graph forensics argument (cut from WIN-012 compression, ~270 words) is structurally a separate argument: it shows that the dome's home-page 'Forensic Zoom' chart is fabricated (L1A unit mismatch, GRACE orbital-mechanics impossibility, no GRACE-processing code in dome repo, r=0.992/AI=1.152 statistics displayed but never computed). This deserves either its own WIN or its own section block. Suggest filing ISS-NNN under decider's intake.
